fields.name: access_token
fields.title: Access Token
fields.description: OAuth 2.0 access token

fields.name: token
fields.title: Personal Access Token
fields.description: GitHub personal access token (fine-grained or classic)

replication_config.title: Replication Configuration
replication_config.description: Settings for data replication from GitHub.

fields.name: repositories
fields.title: GitHub Repositories
fields.description: List of GitHub organizations/repositories, e.g. `airbytehq/airbyte` for single repository, `airbytehq/*` for all repositories from organization

fields.name: start_date
fields.title: Start Date
fields.description: UTC date and time in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ from which to start replicating data.

custom_description:
GitHub is a platform for version control and collaborative software development
using Git. This connector provides access to repositories, branches, commits, issues,
pull requests, reviews, comments, releases, discussions, organizations, teams, and users for
development workflow analysis and project management insights.
supported_questions:
- Show me all open issues in my repositories this month
- List the top 5 repositories I've starred recently
- Analyze the commit trends in my main project over the last quarter
- Find all pull requests created in the past two weeks
- Search for repositories related to machine learning in my organizations
- Compare the number of contributors across my different team projects
- Identify the most active branches in my main repository
- Get details about the most recent releases in my organization
- List all milestones for our current development sprint
- Show me insights about pull request review patterns in our team
- List all unanswered discussions in a repository
- Show me recent discussions in the General category
- Create a new issue titled 'Fix login bug' in my repository
- Create an issue with labels 'bug' and 'urgent' in owner/repo
- File a new bug report issue in our project repository
- Create an issue and assign it to a team member
- Open a new feature request issue in the repository
- Close issue #42 in owner/repo as completed
- Reopen issue #15 in our repository
- Add the 'bug' and 'urgent' labels to issue #10
- Assign user @johndoe to issue #25 in owner/repo
- Update the title of issue #30 to 'New title'
- Add a comment to issue #5 saying 'This has been fixed in the latest release'
- Post a comment on pull request #100 with a status update
- Create a pull request from feature-branch to main in owner/repo
- Open a draft PR titled 'Add new feature' from my-branch to main
unsupported_questions:
- Delete an old branch from the repository
- Schedule a team review for this code
- Merge a pull request
- Delete an issue or comment

issues.description:
Creates a new issue in the specified repository.
Any user with pull access to a repository can create an issue.
Labels and assignees are silently dropped if the authenticated user does not have push access.

issues.description:
Updates an existing issue in the specified repository.
Use this to close/reopen issues, change title/body, add/remove labels, assign users, or set milestones.
Any user with push access can update an issue.

comments.description:
Creates a comment on the specified issue.
This endpoint works for both issues and pull requests, since pull requests are issues.
Any user with read access can create a comment.

comments.description:
Gets information about a specific issue comment by its GraphQL node ID.

Note: This endpoint requires a GraphQL node ID (e.g., 'IC_kwDOBZtLds6YWTMj'),
not a numeric database ID. You can obtain node IDs from the Comments_List response,
where each comment includes both 'id' (node ID) and 'databaseId' (numeric ID).

Gets information about the currently authenticated user.
This is useful when you don't know the username but need to access
the current user's profile, permissions, or associated resources.

viewer_repositories.description:
Returns a list of repositories owned by the authenticated user.
Unlike Repositories_List which requires a username, this endpoint
automatically lists repositories for the current authenticated user.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path within the repository (e.g. 'README.md' or 'src/main.py')

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The git ref to read from — branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to 'HEAD' (default branch)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional array of field names to select

file_content.description:
Returns the text content of a file at a specific path and git ref (branch, tag, or commit SHA).
Only works for text files. Binary files will have text as null and isBinary as true.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The directory path within the repository (e.g. 'src' or 'airbyte-integrations/connectors/source-stripe')

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The git ref — branch name, tag, or commit SHA. Defaults to 'HEAD' (default branch)

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Optional array of field names to select

directory_content.description:
Returns a list of files and subdirectories at a specific path in the repository.
Each entry includes the name, type (blob for files, tree for directories), and object ID.
Use this to explore repository structure before reading specific files.

fields.name: name

fields.description: Issue state in the cache: lowercase `open` or `closed`

fields.description: Reason the issue is in its current state (e.g. `completed`, `not_planned`, `reopened`). Cached values are lowercase.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the issue was created

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the issue was last updated

fields.name: closedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the issue was closed, if applicable

fields.name: locked
fields.description: Whether the conversation on the issue is locked

fields.description: Permalink to the issue on GitHub

fields.description: Label name

fields.description: Label color as a 6-character hex string without a leading `#`

fields.name: url

fields.name: url
fields.description: Permalink to the project on GitHub

fields.name: closed
fields.description: Whether the project has been closed

fields.name: public
fields.description: Whether the project is publicly visible

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the project was created

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the project was last updated

fields.name: id
fields.description: GraphQL node ID of the pull request

fields.name: databaseId
fields.description: REST API numeric identifier for the pull request

fields.name: number
fields.description: Repository-scoped pull request number

fields.name: title
fields.description: Pull request title

fields.name: state
fields.description: Pull request state in the cache: lowercase `open` or `closed` (REST API has no `merged` state; check `mergedAt` to distinguish merged PRs)

fields.name: isDraft
fields.description: Whether the pull request is still a draft

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the pull request was created

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the pull request was last updated

fields.name: closedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the pull request was closed, if applicable

fields.name: mergedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the pull request was merged, if applicable

fields.name: url
fields.description: Permalink to the pull request on GitHub

fields.name: id
fields.description: GraphQL node ID of the release

fields.name: databaseId
fields.description: REST API numeric identifier for the release

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the release

fields.name: tagName
fields.description: Git tag the release points at (e.g. `v1.2.3`)

fields.name: description
fields.description: Markdown body / release notes

fields.name: publishedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the release was published

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the release was created

fields.name: isPrerelease
fields.description: Whether the release is marked as a pre-release

fields.name: isDraft
fields.description: Whether the release is still a draft and not published

fields.name: url
fields.description: Permalink to the release on GitHub

fields.name: id
fields.description: GraphQL node ID of the repository

fields.name: name
fields.description: Short repository name (without owner)

fields.name: nameWithOwner
fields.description: Fully-qualified `owner/name` identifier for the repository

fields.name: description
fields.description: Short description of the repository

fields.name: url
fields.description: Canonical GitHub URL for the repository

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the repository was created

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the repository was last updated

fields.name: pushedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent push to the repository

fields.name: forkCount
fields.description: Number of forks of the repository

fields.name: stargazerCount
fields.description: Number of users who have starred the repository

fields.name: isPrivate
fields.description: Whether the repository is private

fields.name: isFork
fields.description: Whether the repository is a fork of another repository

fields.name: isArchived
fields.description: Whether the repository has been archived

fields.name: id
fields.description: GraphQL node ID of the review

fields.name: databaseId
fields.description: REST API numeric identifier for the review

fields.name: state
fields.description: Review state in the cache: `PENDING`, `COMMENTED`, `APPROVED`, `CHANGES_REQUESTED`, or `DISMISSED`

fields.name: body
fields.description: Review body text

fields.name: submittedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the review was submitted

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the review was created

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the review was last updated

fields.name: url
fields.description: Permalink to the review on GitHub

fields.name: starredAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the user starred the repository

fields.name: name
fields.description: Tag name (e.g. `v1.2.3`)

fields.name: id
fields.description: GraphQL node ID of the team

fields.name: databaseId
fields.description: REST API numeric identifier for the team

fields.name: slug
fields.description: URL-friendly slug for the team within its organization

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the team

fields.name: description
fields.description: Short description of the team

fields.name: privacy
fields.description: Team visibility: `secret` or `closed` (REST API values)

fields.name: url
fields.description: Permalink to the team on GitHub

fields.name: id
fields.description: GraphQL node ID of the user

fields.name: databaseId
fields.description: REST API numeric identifier for the user

fields.name: login
fields.description: User login/handle

fields.name: url
fields.description: Permalink to the user's profile on GitHub
